2025-03-24.abstract: The legislature impeached Han Duck-soo in December, soon after impeaching President Yoon Suk Yeol for declaring martial law. Mr. Yoon’s fate is still unclear.

2025-03-24.title: For Russia, Trump Has a Lot to Offer, Even Without a Ukraine Deal
2025-03-24.abstract: Moscow sees economic and geopolitical benefits in humoring President Trump’s push for a cease-fire in Ukraine. But the Kremlin’s war aims haven’t shifted.

2025-03-24.title: Lebanon, Ravaged by War, Needs Changes to Unlock Aid. That Could Be a Tall Order.
2025-03-24.abstract: For years, the country has failed to enact financial and governance overhauls required by lenders. The recent fighting has made that problem urgent.

2025-03-24.title: To Him, Americans Were Always Heroes. He’s Not So Sure About Today’s.
2025-03-24.abstract: A tour led by an 88-year-old guide in Bastogne, Belgium, scene of a critical battle of World War II, offers a snapshot into the way President Trump’s second term is shifting perceptions of America abroad.

2025-03-24.title: Trump Did What Castros Couldn’t: Take Radio Martí Off the Air
2025-03-24.abstract: For four decades, a U.S.-financed broadcaster provoked the ire of the communist government in Cuba. President Trump dismantled it in a matter of days.

2025-03-25.title: U.N. to Pull International Workers From Gaza Amid Israeli Strikes
2025-03-25.abstract: The United Nations is withdrawing about one-third of its international work force in Gaza, with the reduction coming after an Israeli tank shell hit a U.N. compound.

2025-03-25.title: China Frees Employees of U.S. Consulting Firm After 2-Year Detention
2025-03-25.abstract: The release of the five employees, detained during a crackdown on foreign due diligence, comes as Beijing is trying to bring back overseas investment.

2025-03-25.title: What Makes Sydney’s New Beach Different? It’s 50 Miles Inland.
2025-03-25.abstract: It’s no Bondi Beach, but Pondi, or Penrith Beach, has been a welcome relief to the city’s sweltering western suburbs.

2025-03-25.title: Germany’s New Parliament Is Sworn In, but Where Are the Women?
2025-03-25.abstract: The class of lawmakers taking office is noticeably more male and less diverse than the constituents it will represent.

2025-03-25.title: How a Cheap Drone Punctured Chernobyl’s 40,000-Ton Shield
2025-03-25.abstract: The steel shell that encloses the site of the world’s worst nuclear disaster was built to endure for a century. But war was a scenario its engineers never envisioned.

2025-03-26.title: Art Expert Accused of Duping Prince and Palace of Versailles Stands Trial
2025-03-26.abstract: A connoisseur of 18th-century French furniture fooled buyers into purchasing chairs with fake royal pedigrees, authorities say. The distinct taste of licorice helped give him away.

2025-03-26.title: Dolphin Hunting Is Their Tradition. Rising Seas Have Made It a Lifeline.
2025-03-26.abstract: The residents of Fanalei Island, in the Solomon Islands, say the lucrative hunts will help them buy land elsewhere and move off their sinking home.

2025-03-26.title: South Korea Battles Its Worst Wildfires on Record
2025-03-26.abstract: At least 26 people are dead, including the pilot of a helicopter that crashed trying to contain blazes that strong winds and dry conditions were helping to spread rapidly.

2025-03-26.title: A Traditional Ramadan Treat Gets a Modern Remake (but Good Luck Getting It)
2025-03-26.abstract: Marook, a sweet bread eaten during Ramadan in Syria, used to be a simple loaf, but now the list of choices can be as long as the lines of customers.

2025-03-26.title: Europe Talks Tough on Military Spending, but Unity Is Fracturing
2025-03-26.abstract: European leaders are struggling to find the money and the political will to replace the bulk of the U.S. contribution to Ukraine and to their own defense.

2025-03-27.title: China Is Courting, and Confronting, U.S. Allies Made Uneasy by Trump
2025-03-27.abstract: Even as China offers itself to nations like Japan as a more reliable partner, its ships are pushing into their neighborhoods — sometimes on the very same day.

2025-03-27.title: As Musk Makes Some Headway in India, He Is Also Suing Its Government
2025-03-27.abstract: Elon Musk’s pursuit of business expansion in the country through Tesla and Starlink is coming at the same time that his X platform is waging a fight over free speech.

2025-03-27.title: Europeans Vow to Stand by Ukraine, but Disagree Over Force Proposal
2025-03-27.abstract: President Emmanuel Macron of France insisted that a “reassurance force” of European troops after the war ends was still on the table. But details remained scarce.

2025-03-27.title: Thursday Briefing
2025-03-27.abstract: Efforts to minimize the damage from a leaked Signal chat.

2025-03-27.title: Trump Threatens Europe and Canada if They Band Together Against U.S.
2025-03-27.abstract: In a late-night social media post, the president said he would impose “large scale” tariffs if Canada and the European Union allied to “do economic harm” to America.

2025-03-28.title: F.B.I. Agents in Southeast Asia Paid for Sex While Police Stood By, Watchdog Says
2025-03-28.abstract: Solicitation of prostitutes took place over several years even as employees were training to combat human trafficking, according to a document released to The Times in a lawsuit.

2025-03-28.title: Pope Francis Came Near Death in Hospital, His Doctor Says
2025-03-28.abstract: In an interview, the leader of Pope Francis’s medical team called it “a miracle” that the 88-year-old pontiff left the hospital, but said he needs to change his vigorous habits, at least for a while, to recover.

2025-03-28.title: In Japan, an Iceless Lake and an Absent God Sound an Ancient Warning
2025-03-28.abstract: For centuries, residents in central Japan have chronicled a mysterious natural phenomenon in winter. They see its disappearance as a bad omen.

2025-03-28.title: For Some Autocrats, Even Rigged Elections Can Be Too Much of a Threat
2025-03-28.abstract: Protests are raging in Turkey after the arrest of the country’s most prominent opposition politician.

2025-03-28.title: Here are the latest developments.
2025-03-28.abstract: People in Mandalay, near the epicenter of the 7.7.-magnitude quake, were rushed to the main hospital. Shaking was felt hundreds of miles away in Bangkok, in neighboring Thailand.

2025-03-29.title: ‘A Great Emboldening’: Trump Inspires Wannabe Authoritarians Everywhere
2025-03-29.abstract: President Trump has turned a central precept of American diplomacy on its head, embracing fellow leaders who abandon democratic principles.

2025-03-29.title: Hold the Chianti: Tariff Threat Leaves Italian Bottles Grounded
2025-03-29.abstract: For now, President Trump’s 200 percent tariff on wine remains just a threat. But jittery American wine importers have already hit pause on orders from Tuscany.

2025-03-29.title: China has sent a search-and-rescue team to Myanmar.
In Bangkok, a Desperate Search for Survivors Buried Under Rubble
2025-03-29.abstract: Scores of people are missing in the ruins of a high-rise building that was felled by the quake in Myanmar. Signs of life raised hopes on Saturday, but there was no update on survivors.

2025-03-30.title: French Schools Will Finally Teach Sex Education
2025-03-30.abstract: For 25 years, France has said schools must teach sex ed. Now the government is at last putting a curriculum in place.

2025-03-30.title: Key Takeaways From America’s Secret Military Partnership With Ukraine
2025-03-30.abstract: An investigation by The New York Times has revealed that America was woven into the war far more than previously known.

2025-03-30.title: ‘They Will Label Us as Spies’: The Afghan Students Abandoned by America
2025-03-30.abstract: Students at the American University of Afghanistan in Qatar fear having to return to their Taliban-ruled homeland after aid and visa cutoffs by the Trump administration.

2025-03-30.title: Haiti Doesn’t Make Guns. So How Are Gangs Awash in Them?
2025-03-30.abstract: Federal authorities in several countries have recently nabbed several caches of weapons headed to Haiti, but armed violence continues to rise there, with gangs increasingly obtaining high-caliber firearms and ammunition.

2025-03-30.title: In Bangkok, Looking at High-Rises With a New Sense of Dread
2025-03-30.abstract: Buildings are rising all over the city, emblems of economic growth. But an earthquake that sent one crashing to the ground has stirred fears about building safety.

2025-03-31.title: As Tensions Escalate Between Trump and Europe, Meloni Is Caught in the Middle
2025-03-31.abstract: Each new crisis, whether over Ukraine or tariffs, has made the Italian prime minister’s balancing act that much harder.

2025-03-31.title: Zimbabwe’s Leader Faces Call for Removal From Within His Own Party
2025-03-31.abstract: Nearly eight years after the coup that brought him to power, President Emmerson Mnangagwa is under threat from opponents within his governing ZANU-PF party, who have urged mass protests.

2025-03-31.title: How Trump Supercharged Distrust, Driving U.S. Allies Away
2025-03-31.abstract: Trust is very hard to build and easy to destroy. America and its partners are caught in a spiral of distrust.

2025-03-31.title: Netanyahu, Standing Trial for Corruption, Summoned for Questioning in 2nd Case
2025-03-31.abstract: The war in Gaza has often distracted from an internal battle between Benjamin Netanyahu, the Israeli prime minister, and other parts of the state. On Monday, that domestic conflict careened into full view.

2025-03-31.title: 3 U.S. Soldiers Found Dead in Lithuania After Their Vehicle Sank in a Swamp
2025-03-31.abstract: The soldiers were in a vehicle that became trapped in a bog during a training mission last week. A fourth soldier was still missing.
